name: Advanced Engineering Materials Ltd
description: A leading manufacturer of high-performance turbine components for the aerospace and power generation sectors. Partner since 2025 for surface integrity assessment of nickel-based superalloys.

name: National Physical Laboratory
description: The UK's national metrology institute, collaborating on the development of reference samples and calibration standards for electron microscopy-based defect characterisation.

name: University of Sheffield
description: Collaboration with the Department of Materials Science and Engineering on fatigue crack growth studies in titanium alloys using TEM and in-situ mechanical testing.

name: Rolls-Royce plc
description: Joint research into surface defect analysis of additively manufactured turbine blades, focusing on lack-of-fusion defects and surface roughness effects on fatigue life.

name: Imperial College London
description: Partnership on machine learning approaches for automated defect classification, with a focus on unsupervised anomaly detection in electron microscopy image datasets.
